content.problemStatement:
This problem has subtasks.

You are given two integers LLL and RRR (L‚â§RL \leq RL‚â§R).

You want to purify each integer in this range. To purify an integer xxx between LLL and RRR, you must pay a cost, which is computed as follows:


Choose a positive integer kkk and some integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aka_1, a_2, \ldots, a_ka1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,ak‚Äã such that:

L‚â§ai‚â§RL \leq a_i \leq RL‚â§ai‚Äã‚â§R for each iii.
lcm(a1,a2,‚Ä¶,ak)=x\text{lcm}(a_1, a_2, \ldots, a_k) = xlcm(a1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,ak‚Äã)=x


The cost of purifying xxx using these integers equals max‚Å°(a1,a2,‚Ä¶,ak)\max(a_1, a_2, \ldots, a_k)max(a1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,ak‚Äã).
For example, if L=12L = 12L=12 and R=74R = 74R=74, to purify x=60x = 60x=60,


You can choose the sequence (20,30,20,60)(20, 30, 20, 60)(20,30,20,60) with cost 606060; or
You can choose the sequence (20,30)(20, 30)(20,30) with cost 303030; or
You can choose the sequence (12,20)(12, 20)(12,20) with cost 202020.
However, you cannot choose the sequence (3,4,5,12)(3, 4, 5, 12)(3,4,5,12), because even though its lcm equals 606060, it uses integers <L\lt L<L.
Find the minimum cost to purify every integer from LLL to RRR (inclusive of LLL and RRR).

You must answer TTT independent test cases.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line, containing two space-separated integers LLL and RRR.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum cost to purify all numbers from LLL to RRR (inclusive of LLL and RRR).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: With L=2L = 2L=2 and R=6R = 6R=6,

The cost of purifying 222 will be 222, by choosing the sequence (2)(2)(2).
The cost of purifying 333 will be 333, by choosing the sequence (3)(3)(3).
The cost of purifying 444 will be 444, by choosing the sequence (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4).
The cost of purifying 555 will be 555, by choosing the sequence (5)(5)(5).
The cost of purifying 666 will be 333, by choosing the sequence (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3).

The total cost of purification will be 2+3+4+5+3=172+3+4+5+3=172+3+4+5+3=17
Test case 222: The costs of purifying the numbers from 333 to 121212 are: [3,4,5,6,7,8,9,10,11,4][3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 4][3,4,5,6,7,8,9,10,11,4]. Their sum is 676767.
